mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly boosting equity valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.5 percentage points within a single month, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger recession fears, depressing equity prices over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the predictive Rank IC drops below -0.05 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous contraction in industrial production and a sharp drop in real personal savings rates indicate a supply-demand mismatch that compresses corporate margins, leading to negative equity returns over the following month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns in more than 50% of out-of-sample instances, or the combined Rank IC exceeds 0.02 over any 12-month period.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts act as a leading indicator for consumer spending and economic growth, but only when real interest rates are below 2%, ensuring that borrowing costs do not offset the positive demand signal.
failure_condition: The signal predicts positive returns but SPX forward returns are negative in > 55% of cases when housing starts rise, or the conditional IC drops below 0.01 during high-rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: Sequential quarterly growth in real GDP directly expands the earnings base for S&P 500 constituents, generating positive forward returns over the subsequent 60 trading days as earnings revisions propagate through markets.
failure_condition: Forward returns are not significantly positive when GDP growth exceeds 2% annualized, or the rolling Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When the CPI index exceeds 3.5% year-over-year growth, persistent inflation erodes real purchasing power and forces prolonged monetary tightening, which compresses equity valuations over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in > 60% of instances where CPI exceeds the threshold, or the predictive power vanishes when core inflation is below 3.0%.

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate combined with declining real personal savings rates signals a consumption-driven recession that rapidly deteriorates corporate cash flows, producing negative equity returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns in more than 50% of out-of-sample instances, or the combined Rank IC exceeds 0.02 over any 12-month period.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth boosts equity returns only when it occurs alongside stable or falling unemployment, indicating productive capacity expansion rather than labor market overheating that triggers inflationary policy responses.
failure_condition: The signal predicts positive returns but SPX forward returns are negative in > 55% of cases when industrial production rises, or the conditional IC drops below 0.01 during labor shortages.
